title: Surah Al-A'raf 190–200 — Idols, Character, and Refuge
arabic: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجَاهِلِينَ
translation: Take what is given freely, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.
source: Qur'an Club Lesson 211 · SimplyIslam.sg — Tafsir Surah Al-A'raf 190–200
takeaways: Verse 190: a human weakness — we turn to Allah in need, then once the blessing arrives (like a healthy child) we credit other causes and let gratitude fade.

Verses 191–198: idols are utterly helpless — they create nothing, are themselves created, and lack even the feet, hands, eyes, and ears their own worshippers have; the Prophet ﷺ is told to rely on Allah alone.
Verse 199 — the Golden Rule of character: take what is given freely (pardon people's shortcomings), enjoin what is good ('urf), and turn away from the ignorant rather than argue endlessly.
Verse 200: when turning away stirs anger or the urge for revenge, that is Satan's whisper (waswasah) — seek refuge in Allah (isti'adhah), for He is Hearing and Knowing.
reflection: When someone tests your patience, verse 199 says walk away with your dignity intact — and the moment you feel the urge to bite back, verse 200 says that is exactly when to seek refuge in Allah.

selfTest.question: Verse 199 gives a three-part formula for good character. What are the three parts?
selfTest.answer: Take what is given freely (pardon people's shortcomings), enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.
